content.explanation:
🏷️ What are Labels?

When you build forms, users need to know what each input field is for.

This is where <label> comes in.

🌐 Why Labels Matter

  • They describe input fields clearly
  • They improve accessibility for screen readers
  • They make forms easier to click and use on mobile

♿ Accessibility Idea

Without labels, users (especially screen reader users) may not understand what each input means.

Good labels = professional and inclusive websites.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Label + Input Connection

<label for="username">Username</label>
<input id="username" type="text">

📌 How it works

  • for links label to input
  • id connects input to label

🧠 Key Rule

👉 Every important input should have a label

👉 Labels improve usability + accessibility

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
Registration Form

Please fill in your details:

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Make your form professional

Inside the <form>:

1. Add a label for username
2. Add a label for email
3. Connect each label using 'for' and matching 'id'

Example:
<label for="username">Username</label>
<input id="username" type="text">

🔥 Make your form accessible and clean
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Remember:
label for="name"
input id="name"

They must match exactly

quiz_engine.question: What is the main purpose of using a <label> in HTML forms?
options: To style input boxes with colors
To describe and connect inputs for better usability and accessibility
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 Labels help users understand what each input field means and improve accessibility for screen readers and mobile usability.

takeaways: They connect to inputs using 'for' and 'id'
They improve accessibility for all users

cheat_sheet.accessibility: Making websites usable for everyone, including screen readers
